title: arra-oracle-discord access.json
description: Access control for the arra-oracle-discord channel. Managed by .discord/access-ctl.ts — never hand-edit. This schema gives editors autocomplete + inline type errors (the JSON equivalent of viem's TS types).

group.description: Per-channel policy. Only requireMention + allowFrom are honored (chaiklang PR#2807 guard).

requireMention.description: true = only @-mentions get through (rest dropped, unseen). false = every allowlisted message is answered. 'observe' = non-mentions are delivered as context (meta mode:"observe") but never answered — the bot reads the room, replies only when @-mentioned/replied-to.

allowFrom.description: User ids allowed to drive the bot here. [] = EVERYONE (empty = allow-all, not deny-all).

dmPolicy.description: DM handling. 'disabled' is a GLOBAL master-off — it drops EVERYTHING including guild channels, not just DMs.

allowFrom.description: Top-level DM allowlist (snowflakes). [] = no DMs get through under allowlist policy.

groups.description: Guild channels the bot is active in, keyed by CHANNEL id (not guild id). Use '*' for a catch-all.

pending.description: In-flight pairing codes (managed at runtime; leave {}).

mentionPatterns.description: Extra regexes that count as a mention (top-level, not per-group).

ackReaction.description: Read-receipt emoji reacted on EVERY delivered message (observe included). Unset = default 👀. Empty string disables.

replyToMode.description: Which reply chunks get Discord's reply reference.

textChunkLimit.description: Max chars per outbound message before splitting (default 2000).

chunkMode.description: Split long replies by hard char count or paragraph boundary.

autoThread.description: Thread-on-request: @-mention + the word 'thread'/'เธรด' (or '/thread <name>') → the bot opens a thread and replies inside it. Plain mentions reply in-channel.
